run.jd_text:
# Agentic AI Engineer — Deccan AI

**Location:** Remote-first, team hubs in Bengaluru + SF.
**Level:** Senior individual contributor (staff-level trajectory).
**Team:** Joining a 6-person team building production agent systems for marketplaces and expert networks.

## The role

We're building agent infrastructure that matches expert talent to high-stakes work — interest modeling, simulated outreach, evidence-cited scoring. You'll own end-to-end systems: from prompt architecture and model routing to eval harnesses and the production agents themselves.

Concretely you will:

- Design and ship multi-agent pipelines (planner / reactor / verifier patterns) against frontier LLMs
- Build evaluation harnesses that catch regressions before they ship
- Run inference-optimization work: model selection per stage, context engineering, cost/latency tradeoffs
- Partner with product on rubric design and calibration
- Ship under real constraints (latency budgets, public deploys, observable systems)

## Must-haves

- 5+ years of software engineering with at least 2 years shipping production ML/AI systems
- Demonstrated work with modern LLM tooling: agent frameworks, eval harnesses, RAG pipelines, or tool-use patterns
- Production ownership of a non-trivial system from prompt design through deploy (portfolio, OSS, or prior role)
- Comfort with ambiguity — this role is heavier on judgment than on executing a spec

## Nice-to-haves

- Published writing on agent systems, evals, or prompt engineering
- Background with distributed systems at scale
- Experience with structured-output generation (JSON mode, grammars, constrained decoding)
- Familiarity with Vercel / Next.js / TypeScript for the product surfaces
- OSS contributions to agent frameworks (LangGraph, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, Pydantic AI, etc.)

## What 6 months in looks like

- You've shipped at least one production agent pipeline that we evaluate and trust
- You've raised the floor on our eval harness — we catch regressions we previously missed
- You're routinely the person who answers *"why is this model the right choice for this stage?"* — with data

## What this role is not

- Not a research role — we ship
- Not a people-management role
- Not a prototype factory — we care about production reliability, cost, and latency

---

Plumb (the recruiting intelligence product you're reading this on) is the first product our team shipped. This role is to build the next one.

jd_parsed.archetype: agentic AI engineer building production multi-agent systems
red_flags: Candidates primarily focused on research rather than shipping production systems
Candidates without production LLM/AI deployment experience
Candidates uncomfortable operating without clear specifications

must_haves.description: 5+ years of software engineering experience with at least 2 years shipping production ML/AI systems
Demonstrated work with modern LLM tooling including agent frameworks, eval harnesses, RAG pipelines, or tool-use patterns
Production ownership of a non-trivial AI system from prompt design through deployment (via portfolio, OSS, or prior role)

Experience building evaluation harnesses for LLM/agent systems

Background with distributed systems at scale
Experience with structured-output generation (JSON mode, grammars, constrained decoding)
OSS contributions to agent frameworks (LangGraph, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, Pydantic AI, etc.)

implicit_signals: Strong preference for candidates who can demonstrate concrete shipped agent work via public portfolio or OSS
Role requires high judgment and autonomy in ambiguous environments (startup-style ownership)
Heavy emphasis on production reliability, cost, and latency over research or prototyping
Remote-first with expectation of overlap with Bengaluru + SF hubs
Staff-level trajectory implies potential for technical leadership without people management
success_criteria: Shipped at least one production agent pipeline that the team evaluates and trusts
Significantly improved the eval harness so that regressions are caught before shipping
Routinely able to justify model and architecture choices for specific pipeline stages with data

highlights: Built a RAG document-search pipeline from scratch serving 10M+ docs with sub-200ms p99 latency
Owned end-to-end ML infra including fine-tuning embedding models, eval frameworks, and customer-facing React dashboards
Led migration of the notification delivery service from Node.js to Rust, cutting latency by 40%
Built internal A/B testing tooling used by 30+ product teams

recent_signals.content: Published 'Moving Beyond Vibe Coding' arguing for structured agent architectures and deterministic evals in production systems
Merged PR adding Model Context Protocol (MCP) support to repo-sherpa, enabling IDE integration
Three years of building at a startup taught me that velocity without direction is just exhaustion. Ready to join a team with real infrastructure and a north star.

writing_samples.title: Why We Ditched Our Orchestration Layer
writing_samples.excerpt: After six months fighting abstraction leaks in off-the-shelf LLM frameworks, we rewrote our agent loop in 400 lines of Python. Latency dropped 60% and we could finally reason about token flow.

writing_samples.title: Evals Are User Interfaces
writing_samples.excerpt: Treating evaluation as a batch script is why teams ship broken retrieval. We built a live playground where product managers could grade answers, turning evals into a collaborative interface.

stated_preferences.remote_preference: Hybrid in San Francisco; open to NYC

They are actively in the market and seeking exactly the kind of production AI engineering work described. While they have a question about roadmap stability, they are still eager to proceed with a technical intro. This suggests the candidate is seriously considering the opportunity and is a strong potential fit.

candidates.next_action_draft:
Hi Jules, thanks for the thoughtful conversation. I especially appreciated you sharing that you want to work closer to the agent loop and ship durable systems rather than demos, and your question about what ownership looks like in the first 90 days. Those both showed how aligned this role feels with what you're looking for next. 

I'd like to get you on a 30-minute call with our hiring manager to dive into the roadmap stability and how we treat evals as real engineering. Would Tuesday at 11am PT or Thursday at 2pm PT work for you? Let me know what fits and I'll send the calendar invite right away.

recent_signals.content: Pushed 12 commits to prismlabs/claude-courier: added support for multi-file edit sessions and Anthropic tool-use API
After 6 months of dogfooding, we open-sourced our internal Claude tool. 1k+ stars in a week. Frontend engineers should be building AI interfaces, not just consuming them.
Published 'Why I Stopped Waiting for the Backend Team to Build AI Features'

writing_samples.title: From React to Agents: Shipping a Claude-Powered Code Reviewer
writing_samples.excerpt: We started with a simple Chrome extension for our own team. Six months later, it had replaced our manual PR review checklist and accumulated 1,400 GitHub stars. Here's what I learned about designing interfaces for uncertainty.

candidates.next_action_draft: Hi Jin, thanks again for the thoughtful conversation. While your experience at Prism Labs has strong overlap with production AI systems, the role does require 2+ years of hands-on production ML/AI systems experience which is a bit of a stretch from your background. That said, we're still very interested because of how closely your questions about ownership, eval rigor, agent loops, and roadmap stability align with the kind of engineer we need. Your clear motivation to ship durable systems rather than demos makes you a great potential fit despite the gap. Would you be open to a short 20-minute informal chat with the hiring manager next week so we can explore those topics and give you a clearer picture of the team?

No substantive next steps are embraced beyond receiving a summary and keeping in touch. This profile fits someone not actively on the market and only mildly open to outreach.

candidates.next_action_draft: Thanks for the thoughtful conversation, Maya. I completely understand that the timing isn't right given how happy you are at Anthropic right now. I'll keep you updated occasionally with what our team is working on in agent reliability and production AI systems. Please feel free to reach out anytime your situation changes or if something sparks your interest.

Hi Aisha, thanks for the thoughtful conversation. I appreciated how directly you asked about whether these agents are customer-facing and how you zeroed in on the importance of engineering-led work and eval loops. It’s clear you’re genuinely interested in roles that prioritize reliability and ownership, which aligns perfectly with what we’re building at Deccan.

I’d like to move you forward with a 30-minute call with our hiring manager. He can address your questions around technical depth, failure modes, and production standards in detail.

Would next Tuesday at 11am PT or Thursday at 2pm PT work for you? Let me know and I’ll send over a short technical brief ahead of the call.

writing_samples.title: Why Every ML Team Needs a Skeptic

writing_samples.excerpt: We obsess over compute scaling, but the marginal return on investing in junior researchers compounds faster than adding another 1000 GPUs.
